2606.06942.title: Didact: A Cross-Domain Capability Discovery System for Defence
2606.06942.paper_summary: Policymakers in defence and defence-aligned sectors must monitor rapidly evolving research alongside sector priorities relevant to operational and strategic needs. In practice, these sources are fragmented across heterogeneous formats, disjoint repositories, and siloed update streams, making capability discovery slow and difficult to audit. We present Didact, a prototype that integrates publicly available defence reports and policy documents from Australia with a purpose-built knowledge graph derived from Australian research publications. Didact provides natural language conversations for policy-oriented workflows, and leverages a composite retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline. A key feature of Didact is an interactive Evidence Rail that visualises retrieved evidence and source relationships. Our evaluation of the output quality and runtime of Didact highlights its utility. While Didact has been co-developed as an academia-industry project for the Australian context, it is adaptable to other domains where knowledge is similarly fragmented. A demonstration video is available here:
2606.06942.paper_summary_zh: 政策制定者在國防及相關領域必須監控快速發展的研究，並關注與操作和戰略需求相關的行業優先事項。實際上，這些來源在異質格式、分散的資料庫和孤立的更新流中是碎片化的，這使得能力發現變得緩慢且難以審核。我們提出了Didact，一個原型，它整合了來自澳大利亞的公開防務報告和政策文件，以及從澳大利亞研究出版物中衍生的專門知識圖譜。Didact為以政策為導向的工作流程提供自然語言對話，並利用復合檢索增強生成（RAG）管道。Didact的一個關鍵特徵是互動式證據軌道，它可視化檢索到的證據和來源關係。我們對Didact的輸出質量和運行時間的評估突顯了它的實用性。雖然Didact作為一個學術與產業合作項目在澳大利亞背景下共同開發，但它也可以適應其他知識同樣碎片化的領域。演示視頻可在此處查看：
2606.06942.author: Aarya Bodhankar et.al.
2606.06942.authors: Aarya Bodhankar, Aditya Joshi, Bao Gia Doan, Thomas Marchant, Oscar Leslie, Flora Salim
